summary: Matched several sysdolphin baselib HSD devcom routines by filling in DVD/ARAM callback and wake-up logic, moving private devcom internals into a new devcom.static.h, tightening callback/request pointer types, and renaming two devcom globals in symbols.txt to dvdDC and aramDC. The decomp.dev bot reported 6 new full matches totaling +1936 bytes and HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp improved to 95.69%. No human review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed/placeholder devcom functions: HSD_DevComDVDStdCallback, HSD_DevComDVDARAMEndCallback, HSD_DevComDVDMemCallback, HSD_DevComDVDCallback, HSD_DevComDVDWakeUp, and HSD_DevComCancelEx.

Substantially filled in HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp, including ARAM state checks, relay buffer allocation, cache flush/invalidate calls, ARQPostRequest paths for transfer types 3, 0xB, 0x19, 0x1A, and 0x1B, and cancellation handling.

Added devcom.static.h for private devcom implementation details: HSD_DevCom struct definition, DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE/DEVCOMDEST_SBUF constants, ARQRequest arrays, devComStatus, relay buffers, DVDFileInfo fileinfo, dvdDC, aramDC, and other static state.
Reduced devcom.h to public prototypes and removed private helper/callback prototypes and the HSD_DevCom struct definition from the public header.

Changed HSD_DevComRequest source/destination arguments from u32 to uintptr_t and the HSD_DevCom struct source/destination fields in devcom.static.h to intptr_t, better reflecting address-sized values.

Changed HSD_DevComCallback in forward.h from taking a void* first argument to taking an int request id first argument; propagated related callback signature changes in lbfile, lbdvd, grstadium, and synth.
Changed Dolphin ARQ callbacks from u32 pointerToARQRequest to struct ARQRequest* and removed casts in __ARQInterruptServiceRoutine.
Renamed sbss symbols HSD_DevCom_804D77F8 to dvdDC and HSD_DevCom_804D7808 to aramDC in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Made several devcom helper callbacks static in devcom.c, including HSD_DevComUnlink, HSD_DevComStdCallback, and HSD_DevComARAMCallback.
smart_moves: Separating devcom.static.h from devcom.h kept private implementation state local while allowing devcom.c to use exact static data declarations needed for matching.
Using typed ARQRequest* callbacks instead of u32 callback payloads removed noisy casts and aligned ARQ callback call sites with the actual request pointer semantics.
Renaming dvdDC and aramDC in symbols.txt converted anonymous address-based globals into meaningful current-operation names, making the DVD/ARAM scheduler logic easier to read.
The relay-buffer helper getRelayBufIdx centralized the repeated scan/set pattern for devComRelayBufFlag and returned -1 when both 0x4000 buffers were busy.
Preserved interrupt safety around queue mutations and wake-up paths with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, matching the asynchronous DVD/ARAM scheduling model.
Used bool literals for cancelflag/devComRelayBufFlag state where the code semantics were boolean, while keeping u8/u16 storage where required by layout.
decomp_lessons: For callback-heavy subsystems, first correct the callback typedefs and related public prototypes; matching often depends on whether the compiler sees an int, pointer, or uintptr_t at call sites.

Do not expose every guessed helper in public headers. Private static functions and static data can live in a module-specific .static.h when matching requires exact declarations but users only need the public API.
Meaningful names for active global state such as dvdDC and aramDC can clarify control flow enough to finish complex asynchronous matching work.
ARQ/DVD bridge code needs careful cache-operation placement: DCStoreRange before memory-to-ARAM transfers and DCInvalidateRange before ARAM-to-memory or relay-buffer reads.

Chunked transfers may intentionally use different callback paths for intermediate chunks versus final chunks; this PR used HSD_DevComStdCallback for continued chunks and completion callbacks when size fit the remaining transfer.

Cancellation paths are part of matching, not just runtime behavior: pending queue entries, currently active entries, and relay-buffer-backed entries each needed distinct handling in HSD_DevComCancelEx.
Address-valued fields should use uintptr_t/intptr_t where the original code treats them as integer addresses and also performs arithmetic.
naming_conventions: Anonymous devcom globals at fixed addresses remained as HSD_DevCom_804D77F5/HSD_DevCom_804D77F6/etc. when semantics were not fully named.
Two semantically identified globals were renamed from address names to role names: dvdDC and aramDC.
Private helper names followed subsystem prefixes such as HSD_DevComDVDStdCallback, HSD_DevComDVDARAMEndCallback, HSD_DevComDVDMemCallback, and HSD_DevComARAMCallback_inline.
Callback typedef and parameter naming favored cb over callback in HSD_DevComRequest to avoid colliding with the struct field assignment pattern.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in HSD_DevComDVDCallback, indicating a stack-shape matching adjustment.
Used an explicit nested zeroing loop over HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs with a 0x200 outer count and 8 stores per iteration, likely to reproduce the original clear-buffer codegen.
Kept MIN(dvdDC->size, 0x80000) for large direct DVD memory reads and MIN(dvdDC->size, DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE) for relay-buffer DVD reads to mirror original chunk sizes.
Selected callback function pointers conditionally based on whether the transfer exceeded DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE, preserving intermediate versus terminal callback behavior.

Changed relay buffer storage from u8[2][DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE] to int[2][DEVCOM_BUF_SIZE / sizeof(int)] in devcom.static.h, supporting word-wise clearing and pointer arithmetic patterns seen in the matched code.
Made internal devcom callbacks static where they were implementation-only, likely improving symbol/prototype consistency for matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased by +0.05% and +1936 bytes.

decomp.dev bot listed 6 new full matches in main/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom: HSD_DevComDVDCallback, HSD_DevComDVDWakeUp, HSD_DevComCancelEx, HSD_DevComDVDARAMEndCallback, HSD_DevComDVDMemCallback, and HSD_DevComDVDStdCallback.
decomp.dev bot reported HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp improved from 0.00% to 95.69% but was not a full match in this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish the remaining 4.31% of HSD_DevComARAMWakeUp.
Search for uses of devcom.static.h to see whether the private-header pattern is reused in other baselib modules.
Search for PRs changing HSD_DevComCallback call sites after the first parameter became int.
Search for subsequent renames of HSD_DevCom_804D77F5, HSD_DevCom_804D77F6, HSD_DevCom_804D77F7, HSD_DevCom_804D77FC, and HSD_DevCom_804D7804.
Compare ARQCallback typedef changes in this PR against other Dolphin SDK callback typing fixes.
